goal: Make the Celtic Cross poster's bottom reading legible by replacing the cramped insight panels with one flowing narration
done: Replaced the 3-panel drawInsights with drawNarration in share/poster.js: a single reading weaving Present -> Obstacle (challenge) -> Advice -> Outcome (lead sentence each, no labels/card names), large full-contrast italic-serif, gold ornament, shrink-to-fit font (33->23px) so it never truncates. Added buildCelticNarration + the 'challenge' insight to resolveCelticCrossPosterContent.
symptom: Founder couldn't read the reading at the bottom of the Celtic Cross poster.
root_cause: The bottom was 3 insight panels with body text ~12-14px at rgba(255,255,255,0.78) on a 1080px-wide canvas — far too small and low-contrast.
fix: Single narration block: position lead-sentences joined into prose (Present/Obstacle/Advice/Outcome), Cormorant Garamond ~33px (#f7f4ee), shrink-to-fit via wrapTextLines so the full reading always shows.
verified: local canvas render harness (share/poster-preview/celtic-harness.html) via chrome-devtools: full narration renders complete + legible on 1080x1920, no mid-sentence cut after the shrink-to-fit fix
card art CDN-CORS-blocked on localhost (fallback gradients) — text layout, the changed part, verified
affects: Celtic Cross (full mode) share poster, all presets
filed: drawInsightPanel now unused/dead in renderCelticCrossPoster — remove in a cleanup pass
needs Dunkin on-device visual QA with real card art
TH narration connectives are bare sentence-joins — review reads naturally in Thai
notes: Mirror sync deferred (BUG-015). EN 'The Challenge' -> 'The Obstacle' label flip still pending founder confirm (separate from this poster prose).
